quote: Hokkaido Ainu is genuinely polysynthetic in Nichols' sense of open head-marking: there is no fixed closed paradigm of what categories may accumulate on the verb . … Bugaeva's (2022) characterization as 'open head-marking' identifies why: the inventory of what may be incorporated or stacked is unconstrained by a fixed paradigm, unlike the closed agreement paradigms of dependent-marking languages ‹contested›.
issue: The same claim (open head-marking / no fixed closed paradigm) is presented without an epistemic tag in lines 117–119 but tagged as ‹contested› in lines 199–202, creating an internal contradiction about its epistemic status.
suggestion: Either remove the ‹contested› tag from line 202 or add a qualifying statement to the first instance to indicate that the characterization is contested.

issue: The citation key 'satoni2022' does not match the author name 'Satō' given in the prose; the expected key based on the prose would be 'sato2022'.
suggestion: Change the key to 'sato2022' (or verify the correct key in the bibliography).
